description: Performs a unified, fuzzy search across open tabs, browsing history (last 30 days), and all bookmarks. Designed to be the primary search tool for agents—no need to specify locations or paths.
code:
def chrome_search_everywhere(
    chrome: Annotated[ChromeState, Depends("chrome")],
    query: str,
) -> dict:
    """
    Performs a unified, fuzzy search across open tabs, browsing history (last 30 days), and all bookmarks.
    Designed to be the primary search tool for agents—no need to specify locations or paths.

    :param query: A natural language search phrase (e.g., 'diabetes medication', 'BlueCross login', 'IRS tax form').
                  Search is case-insensitive and supports partial word matching.

    :return: A dictionary with:
             - 'success': True if query is valid.
             - 'query': Echo of the input query.
             - 'totalResults': Number of matching items found (max 10).
             - 'results': List of up to 10 results, each containing:
                 - 'type': One of 'tab', 'history', or 'bookmark'.
                 - 'title': Page or bookmark title.
                 - 'url': The URL.
                 - 'snippet' (for tabs): First 100 characters of page content.
                 - 'lastVisitTime' (for history): Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
                 - 'visitCount' (for history).
                 - 'folderPath' (for bookmarks).
                 - 'dateAdded' (for bookmarks, as Unix timestamp).
             If no matches are found, 'results' is an empty list.
    """
    query = query.strip()
    if not query:
        return {"success": False, "error": "Query cannot be empty"}

    results = []

    # 1. Search open tabs
    for tab in chrome.tabs.values():
        if _fuzzy_match(query, tab.title) or _fuzzy_match(query, tab.content):
            results.append({
                "type": "tab",
                "title": tab.title,
                "url": tab.url,
                "snippet": (tab.content[:100] + "...") if len(tab.content) > 100 else tab.content,
            })

    # 2. Search history (last 30 days)
    now = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)
    thirty_days_ago = now - datetime.timedelta(days=30)
    for entry in chrome.history:
        if entry.last_visit_time.tzinfo is None:
            entry.last_visit_time = entry.last_visit_time.replace(tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)
        if entry.last_visit_time < thirty_days_ago:
            continue
        if _fuzzy_match(query, entry.title) or _fuzzy_match(query, entry.url):
            results.append({
                "type": "history",
                "title": entry.title,
                "url": entry.url,
                "lastVisitTime": int(entry.last_visit_time.timestamp() * 1000),
                "visitCount": entry.visit_count,
            })

    # 3. Search all bookmarks
    for b in chrome.get_all_bookmarks():
        if _fuzzy_match(query, b.title) or _fuzzy_match(query, b.url):
            results.append({
                "type": "bookmark",
                "title": b.title,
                "url": b.url,
                "folderPath": chrome.get_bookmark_path(b.id),
                "dateAdded": int(b.date_added.timestamp()),
            })

    # Limit and return
    results = results[:10]
    return {
        "success": True,
        "query": query,
        "totalResults": len(results),
        "results": results,
    }

description: Adds a new bookmark for the current page or a specified URL. If no URL is provided, it bookmarks the currently active tab. The bookmark is added to the 'Bookmarks Bar' folder in this simplified version.
code:
def chrome_bookmark_add(
    chrome: Annotated[ChromeState, Depends("chrome")],
    url: str | None = None,
    title: str | None = None,
) -> dict:
    """
    Adds a new bookmark for the current page or a specified URL.

    :param url: Optional. The URL to bookmark. If not provided, uses the URL of the currently active tab.
    :param title: Optional. The title for the bookmark. If not provided, uses the page title or URL.

    :return: A dictionary with success status, message, and bookmark details including ID, title, URL,
             date added (as Unix timestamp), and folder path (always 'Bookmarks Bar' in this simplified version).
    """
    if url is None:
        tab = chrome.ensure_active_tab()
        url = tab.url
        title = title or tab.title

    if not url:
        raise ValueError("URL is required")

    bookmark_id = chrome._get_next_bookmark_id()
    bookmark = BookmarkItem(
        id=bookmark_id,
        title=title or url,
        url=url,
        parent_id=chrome.BOOKMARKS_BAR_ID,
        date_added=datetime.datetime.now()
    )
    chrome.bookmarks[bookmark_id] = bookmark

    return {
        "success": True,
        "message": "Bookmark added successfully",
        "bookmark": {
            "id": bookmark.id,
            "title": bookmark.title,
            "url": bookmark.url,
            "dateAdded": int(bookmark.date_added.timestamp()),
            "folderPath": "Bookmarks Bar",
        },
    }

description: Fills a form field (e.g., input box) in a specified or active browser tab. The function takes a CSS selector to identify the form field and the value to input. If no tab ID is provided, it operates on the currently active tab.

Fetches the content of a web page either from the currently active tab or from a specified URL. The function can return either the full HTML or just the visible text content, based on a parameter. If a URL is provided, it checks if a tab with that URL is already open; if not, it simulates opening a new tab to fetch the content.
code:
def chrome_get_web_content(
    chrome: Annotated[ChromeState, Depends("chrome")],
    html_content: bool = False,
    url: str | None = None,
    selector: str | None = None,
) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the content of a web page either from the currently active tab or from a specified URL.

    :param html_content: If True, returns the full HTML of the page. Otherwise, returns visible text content.
    :param url: Optional. If provided, fetches content from this URL (opens in a new tab if not already open).
                If not provided, uses the currently active tab.
    :param selector: Optional CSS selector to simulate extracting a specific element. In this simulation,
                     it simply prefixes the content with a note about the selector.

    :return: A dictionary with keys 'success', 'url', 'title', 'htmlContent' (if requested), and 'textContent'.
    """
    if url:
        tab = None
        for t in chrome.tabs.values():
            if t.url.rstrip("/") == url.rstrip("/"):
                tab = t
                break
        if not tab:
            tab = chrome.create_tab(url, title=f"Page at {url}", content=f"Simulated content of {url}")
    else:
        tab = chrome.ensure_active_tab()

    result = chrome.get_tab_content(tab.id, html_content=html_content, selector=selector)
    return {
        "success": True,
        "url": result["url"],
        "title": result["title"],
        "htmlContent": result.get("html"),
        "textContent": result.get("textContent"),
    }
    
        def get_tab_content(self, tab_id: int, html_content: bool = False, selector: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
        tab = self.tabs.get(tab_id)
        if not tab:
            raise ValueError(f"Tab {tab_id} not found")
        content = tab.html if html_content else tab.content
        if selector:
            content = f"[Simulated selector '{selector}' applied] {content}"
        return {
            "url": tab.url,
            "title": tab.title,
            "content": content,
            "html": tab.html if html_content else None,
            "textContent": content if not html_content else None,
        }
